title: Calendar / schedule report
description: Data contract for the calendar-schedule format: a discrete day-cell calendar grid (month / week / resource view) with event chips inside cells, an event-type legend, status semantics, and click-to-detail popovers. This is NOT a continuous gantt time axis (that is timeline-roadmap); here time is quantised into day cells. The sample's #report-data MUST be a valid instance of this schema.

meta.description: Shared report chrome metadata (brand-neutral). Use real or brand-neutral values (e.g. Independent Research / Open Research / Research Desk); never leave a {{...}} placeholder.

reportType.description: Report type label shown in the footer, e.g. 'Release calendar'.

title.description: Calendar title shown in the header, e.g. 'Release & Test Calendar'.

subtitle.description: Optional short standfirst under the title.

date.description: Report generation date (YYYY-MM-DD). Used for footer meta and may seed the 'today' highlight if today is absent.

today.description: Optional explicit 'today' date (YYYY-MM-DD) for the today-cell highlight. If omitted, the renderer uses meta.date, then the real current date.

weekStart.description: First day of the week column: 0 = Sunday, 1 = Monday. Default 1 (Monday).

theme.description: Initial brand theme; sets <html data-theme>. The in-report .rc-theme switcher can change it live.

view.description: Initial view. 'month' = 7-column weeks of day cells; 'week' = a single week's day columns with taller cells; 'resource' = rows are resources, columns are the days of one week (a schedule lane grid). The header view-toggle switches between these live.

periodStart.description: The anchor date (YYYY-MM-DD). In month view the grid shows the calendar month that contains this date; in week/resource view it shows the week that contains this date. Prev/next moves this anchor by one month (month view) or one week (week/resource view).

eventTypes.description: The event categories shown as toggleable legend chips and used to colour-band event chips. 3-4 types recommended. Order is the legend order. Each type is auto-assigned one of the family accent/state palettes by index unless 'tone' is set.

key.description: Stable type key referenced by events[].type, e.g. 'release'.

label.description: Human label shown on the legend chip, e.g. 'Release'.

tone.description: Optional palette tone for this type. 'brand' uses the active accent; 'ok'/'risk'/'hold' use the family semantic palettes; 'accentN' (1-4) picks a fixed neutral-distinct accent so multiple types stay distinguishable. If omitted, a tone is assigned by index.

resources.description: Optional resource lanes (teams / environments / people) for the resource view and the 'resource' grouping. Each event may reference one via events[].resource. Required to be meaningful only when view can be 'resource'.

key.description: Stable resource key referenced by events[].resource, e.g. 'env-stg'.

meta.description: Optional short sub-label, e.g. an owner or capacity note.

events.description: The calendar events. ~15-25 across the event types. Multi-day events (end set) render as a continuous bar across the day cells they span (clipped per week row). All-day vs timed is a presentation hint.

id.description: Unique event id, e.g. 'EV-01'. Referenced by the detail popover.

title.description: Event title shown on the chip and in the popover.

end.description: Optional inclusive end date (YYYY-MM-DD) for multi-day events. Omit or equal to start for single-day events.

type.description: Event-type key; must match one eventTypes[].key. Drives colour band and legend filtering.

kind.description: Optional event sub-kind. 'milestone' renders a single-day event as a diamond/flag gate glyph instead of a bar (independent of type colour and status). Omit for ordinary bar/point events.

status.description: Optional family semantic state, surfaced as a status dot/badge on the chip and in the popover. 'ok' = on track / done, 'risk' = at risk, 'hold' = blocked / on hold.

allDay.description: If true, the chip shows no time and reads as a full-day band. Defaults true when no time component is present.

time.description: Optional display time label for timed events, e.g. '09:30' or '14:00-16:00'. Shown on the chip when allDay is false.

resource.description: Optional resource key (must match resources[].key); places the event in that lane in resource view.

owner.description: Optional owner / assignee name shown in the popover.

note.description: Optional one-line detail shown in the popover body.
